text: This study investigates the attitude of Islamic Shari’ah towards early marriage using qualitative library research approach. By synthesizing the information contained in relevant textual materials, whether primary or secondary, using the method of content analysis, this study examined the tasa wi’, maqa si and manqa bi’ aspects of marriage. Marriage, in Islam, is a means of worshipping Allah and it is built upon the principles of morality. The issue of early marriage (marriage involving the bride or groom who is less than 18 years old) however remains controversial. While there are a number of views from some classical Islamic jurists supporting the validity of the institution of early marriage, a large number of contemporary scholars argue that it is unlawful based on some evidences including the evidence of the Prophet’s marriage to Aisyah when she was six years old. Therefore, this study based on the principles of Islamic law and the contemporary laws of the land described the consequences that young children are exposed to when they are married early. The study therefore recommended that an integrative approach that brings together Islamic legal, political and policy instruments is needed to guarantee the protection of the rights and interests of the children in contemporary times.
